highlights.title: Elon Musk hints at Tesla (TSLA)-SpaceX merger on earnings call
highlights.description:
Elon Musk hinted at a merger between Tesla and SpaceX on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call, pointing to “more and more overlap” between the two companies he controls.
He stopped short of confirming anything, deferring to Tesla’s general counsel and “the appropriate process” — but the exchange is the closest Musk has come to addressing the merger speculation directly.

highlights.title: Tesla and Alphabet shares slump in premarket trading as AI spending concerns spook investors
highlights.description: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

highlights.title: Google is hoarding TPUs to chase artificial general intelligence
highlights.description: Yet the Chocolate Factory is also buying more third-party compute capacity to handle demand for the G-Cloud

highlights.title: Alphabet and Tesla test Wall Street's patience as AI spending overshadows growth
highlights.description: Shares of Alphabet and Tesla dipped after the companies reported massive spending increases in their quarterly earnings reports.

highlights.title: Tesla still has no real plan for Hardware 3 owners promised FSD
highlights.description:
Elon Musk was asked directly on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call whether the company still plans to upgrade Hardware 3 (HW3) cars so they can run its latest “Full Self-Driving” software. His answer made one thing clear: Tesla still has no concrete plan.
Instead of committing to a fix, Musk floated a grab-bag of options and timelines — none of them firm — for the millions of vehicles Tesla sold with the promise that they had “all the hardware necessary” for self-driving.

highlights.title: Khosla Ventures in Talks to Raise $5.5 Billion in New Funds
highlights.description: Early OpenAI backer Khosla Ventures is discussing raising as much as $5.5 billion for its latest set of venture investment funds, a haul that would amount to its largest fundraising event in its two-decade history, according to people familiar with the matter.

highlights.title: Senior White House official claims China’s K3 model stolen from Anthropic
highlights.description: And accuses Thailand hosting hardware that may have helped distillation attack

highlights.title: Chip firm priced as China's most valuable company before its IPO brings scrutiny to crypto platform
highlights.description: The outsized premium was due in part to offshore investors who, locked out of the highly anticipated listings, turned to crypto for a parallel market.

highlights.title: Intel Needs More Than Blowout Earnings as Chips Rally Falters
highlights.description: Intel Corp. is expected to report strong second-quarter earnings after the market close Thursday, but even blowout results likely won’t be enough to reverse the stock’s July slide.

highlights.title: STMicro Plunges as Weak Forecast Eclipses Fourth-Quarter Outlook
highlights.description: STMicroelectronics NV plunged the most in a year after the chipmaker forecast third-quarter sales that missed analysts’ expectations, dimming hopes for a stronger recovery on the back of artificial intelligence data center demand.

business.title: China’s Psibot Becomes Latest AI Startup to Hit $1 Billion Value
business.description: PsiBot is raising close to $100 million of funding at a $1.48 billion valuation, joining a growing roster of Chinese AI startups capitalizing on interest in the burgeoning field.

ai.title: AI Data Center Boom Sparks 80% Surge in First-Half FDI Applications to Thailand
ai.description: Foreign investors applied to invest 1.37 trillion baht ($40.6 billion) in Thailand in the first half of 2026, up 80% from a year earlier, amid a wave of investments in artificial intelligence and data center projects.

ai.title: Live markets: Bitcoin trades above $65,000 as Alphabet's bigger AI bill props up the chip trade
ai.description: Alphabet beat on revenue but raised its AI spending forecast again, sending its stock lower after hours. For the chip complex bitcoin tracks, more spending is the bullish read.

ai.title: Why we're sticking with Alphabet despite an imperfect quarter and more AI spending
ai.description: The market isn't in a forgiving mood, but we're not in a hurry to leave this stock behind.

ai.title: IBM lowers full-year forecast after earnings warning
ai.description: IBM is now looking to improve productivity with artificial intelligence, including with a new coding tool called Bob.

ai.title: China Cuts AI Gap as Moonshot Shows Zhipu Isn’t One-Off, BI Says
ai.description: Chinese AI models narrowed the performance gap with US counterparts to a record-low 6% in June, down from 9% in May, which Bloomberg Intelligence said raises questions about the sustainability of US technological supremacy in the sector.

chips_cloud.title: TSMC Supplier LCY Says KKR to Exit, Allowing Faster Growth
chips_cloud.description: Private equity giant KKR & Co. plans to gradually sell its stake in LCY Group’s business, the Taiwan chemical maker’s chairman said, paving the way for the family-controlled company to accelerate the expansion of its semiconductor material operations.

ev_autonomous.title: Ask me anything about Samsung’s new, wide Galaxy Z Fold 8
ev_autonomous.description: I’m on my way home from Samsung’s big Galaxy Unpacked event and I’ve moved into the new Galaxy Z Fold 8. So, ask me anything!

space.title: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket is expected to crash into the moon this August
space.description: The upper stage of a Falcon 9 rocket has been drifting in space for over a year after it launched. It's expected to crash into the moon next month.
